dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8785 IH: Medium Transit Intensive Cities Authorization Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Carbajal (for himself and Mr. Moore of Utah) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To amend section 5336 of title 49, United States Code, to provide for certain apportionments to medium-sized transit intensive cities, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Medium Transit Intensive Cities Authorization Act of 2026.
Section 5336 of title 49, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (a) by striking subsection (h)(5) and inserting subsection (h)(6);

subparagraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (4) and (5) as paragraphs (5) and (6), respectively;
by inserting after paragraph (3) the following:

paragraph.text: of amounts not apportioned under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3), 1.5 percent shall be apportioned to urbanized areas with populations of at least 200,000 but not more than 999,999 in accordance with subsection (k);

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (6), as so redesignated, by striking and (4) and inserting (4), and (5); and

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: The amount to be apportioned under subsection (h)(4) shall be apportioned among eligible areas in the ratio that—

clause.text: the number of performance categories for which each eligible area meets or exceeds the industry average in urbanized areas with a population of at least 1,000,000; bears to
the aggregate number of performance categories for which all eligible areas meet or exceed the industry average in urbanized areas with a population of at least 1,000,000.

subparagraph.text: The average in urbanized areas with a population of at least 1,000,000 shall—

clause.text: be calculated separately for each such urbanized area for each performance category; and
for each performance category, be the average of each individual performance category calculated as described in clause (i).

subparagraph.text: The Secretary shall calculate apportionments under this subsection for a fiscal year using data from the national transit database used to calculate apportionments for that fiscal year under this section.

The term “eligible area” means an urbanized area with a population of at least 200,000 but not more than 999,999 that meets or exceeds in 1 or more performance categories the industry average for all urbanized areas with a population of at least 1,000,000, as determined by the Secretary in accordance with subsection (c)(1).
The term “performance category” means each of the following:

clause.text: Passenger miles traveled per vehicle revenue mile.
Passenger miles traveled per vehicle revenue hour.
